title: Design System: Copilot Enterprise Spend Simulator

display.purpose: The headline bill readout only — the gauge's needle.

headline.purpose: Secondary readouts: max bill, pool-exhausted day, blocked count.

title.purpose: App/brand name and section titles (with a leading gradient tick).

body.purpose: UI copy and captions; prose capped near 62ch.

label.purpose: Mono uppercase readout labels, meter scales, status chips, the single hero eyebrow.

shadows.name: subtle
shadows.purpose: Slider thumbs and small raised chips.

shadows.name: panel-lift
shadows.purpose: Panels, hero, and cards on hover.

motion.name: state
motion.purpose: Default UI state transitions (buttons, borders, hovers).

motion.name: meter
motion.purpose: Bill-meter segment growth and chart advance as the day steps.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The primary action — indigo gradient with a soft glow.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font: 600 14px/1 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; color: #fff; background: linear-gradient(180deg, var(--brand-strong), var(--brand)); border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--brand) 70%, #000); border-radius: 9px; padding: 7px 13px; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 6px 16px -8px color-mix(in srgb, var(--brand) 80%, transparent); transition: background 0.15s ease, border-color 0.15s ease, transform 0.05s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: linear-gradient(180deg, var(--brand-strong), var(--brand-strong)); border-color: var(--brand-strong); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--brand) 45%, transparent); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Low-emphasis / destructive action; turns coral on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { font: 500 12.5px/1 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; color: var(--muted); background: transparent; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 9px; padding: 6px 9px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease, color 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--limit) 14%, transparent); color: var(--limit); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--brand) 45%, transparent); }

components.name: Panel
components.description: The base surface container, with a section title carrying a leading gradient tick.
components.html:
Enterprise controls

Tonal-layered surface; depth comes from the panel ramp, not a heavy shadow.
components.css: .ds-panel { background: linear-gradient(180deg, color-mix(in srgb, var(--panel-2) 45%, var(--panel)), var(--panel)); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 14px; padding: 18px; box-shadow: 0 10px 30px -12px rgba(2,5,15,0.7); color: var(--text); } .ds-panel-title { font: 600 15px/1.2 'IBM Plex Sans Condensed', 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; letter-spacing: -0.01em; margin: 0 0 10px; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 9px; } .ds-panel-title::before { content: ''; width: 3px; height: 15px; border-radius: 3px; background: linear-gradient(var(--brand), var(--accent)); flex: none; } .ds-panel-body { font: 400 14px/1.5 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; color: var(--text-dim); margin: 0; }

components.name: Range Slider
components.description: Indigo-filled range with a white, indigo-ringed thumb.
components.html: <input type="range" class="ds-range" min="0" max="100" value="45" style="--pos:45%" aria-label="Average monthly usage" />
components.css: .ds-range { -webkit-appearance: none; appearance: none; width: 240px; height: 22px; background: transparent; cursor: pointer; } .ds-range::-webkit-slider-runnable-track { height: 6px; border-radius: 999px; background: linear-gradient(to right, var(--brand) 0 var(--pos, 0%), var(--panel-3) var(--pos, 0%) 100%); } .ds-range::-webkit-slider-thumb { -webkit-appearance: none; appearance: none; width: 16px; height: 16px; margin-top: -5px; border-radius: 50%; background: #fff; border: 3px solid var(--brand); box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(2,5,15,0.4); transition: transform 0.1s ease; } .ds-range:hover::-webkit-slider-thumb { transform: scale(1.12); } .ds-range:focus-visible::-webkit-slider-thumb { box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--brand) 45%, transparent); }

components.name: Switch
components.description: Pill toggle; fills indigo-soft when checked.
components.html: <input type="checkbox" class="ds-switch" checked aria-label="Stop usage at limit" />
components.css: .ds-switch { -webkit-appearance: none; appearance: none; position: relative; width: 34px; height: 20px; border-radius: 999px; background: var(--panel-3); border: 1px solid var(--border); cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.18s ease, border-color 0.18s ease; } .ds-switch::before { content: ''; position: absolute; top: 1px; left: 1px; width: 16px; height: 16px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--muted); transition: transform 0.18s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), background 0.18s ease; } .ds-switch:checked { background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--brand) 16%, transparent); border-color: var(--brand); } .ds-switch:checked::before { transform: translateX(14px); background: var(--brand-strong); } .ds-switch:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--brand) 45%, transparent); }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: Live-simulation pill with a pulsing teal dot.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; font: 500 12px/1 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; color: var(--muted); padding: 6px 12px; background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--panel-2) 70%, transparent); border: 1px solid var(--border-soft); border-radius: 999px; } .ds-chip strong { color: var(--text); font-weight: 600; } .ds-chip-dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--pool); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--pool) 16%, transparent); animation: ds-pulse 2.4s ease-in-out infinite; } @keyframes ds-pulse { 0%, 100% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px color-mix(in srgb, var(--pool) 16%, transparent); } 50% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 5px color-mix(in srgb, var(--pool) 8%, transparent); } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-chip-dot { animation-duration: 0.001ms; } }

components.name: Bill-Composition Meter
components.description: The signature gauge: a license segment and a metered segment fill a ticked track against a coral limit ceiling.
components.html: $0limit $8,200
components.css: .ds-meter { display: grid; gap: 8px; width: 320px; } .ds-meter-track { position: relative; height: 26px; border-radius: 9px; overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid var(--border); background: repeating-linear-gradient(90deg, color-mix(in srgb, var(--text) 12%, transparent) 0 1px, transparent 1px 10%), var(--panel-3); } .ds-meter-seg { position: absolute; top: 0; bottom: 0; transition: width 0.6s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), left 0.6s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-meter-license { background: linear-gradient(180deg, var(--brand-strong), var(--brand)); } .ds-meter-metered { background: linear-gradient(180deg, color-mix(in srgb, var(--metered) 88%, #fff), var(--metered)); } .ds-meter-ceiling { position: absolute; top: -3px; bottom: -3px; width: 2px; background: var(--limit); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--limit) 22%, transparent); } .ds-meter-scale { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; font: 500 11px/1 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; color: var(--muted); } .ds-meter-ceil { color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--limit) 85%, var(--text)); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-meter-seg { transition-duration: 0.001ms; } }

narrative.overview: This is a precision spend instrument, not a dashboard. Every decision serves one act: reading a bill as it forms. The surface is a deep-ink navy cockpit tinted toward indigo, over which panels rise through tonal layering rather than shadow. Numbers are the material: ledger-grade tabular monospace so digits never jitter while the day 1 to 30 charts trace the credit pool draining and the metered overage climbing toward a hard limit. The palette is information, not decoration: five semantic hues carry fixed meaning everywhere they appear. It rejects the generic SaaS dashboard, the flashy consumer/marketing site, and the intimidating raw spreadsheet in equal measure.
keyCharacteristics: Deep-ink navy cockpit with indigo-tinted neutrals; depth from tonal layering, not heavy shadow.
A fixed, information-coded five-color semantic system (pool / metered / limit / Business / Enterprise).
Ledger-grade tabular-mono numerals for every value that changes during the simulation.
IBM Plex Sans Condensed (display) + IBM Plex Sans (UI) + IBM Plex Mono (data), drawn from one engineered superfamily and separated by width and weight.
One signature component — the bill-composition meter — that everything else supports.

rules.name: The Fixed-Meaning Rule
rules.body: The five semantic hues are never used decoratively. Amber always means metered, coral always means blocked, teal always means the pool. If a value isn't one of those things, it doesn't get that color.

rules.name: The No-Color-Alone Rule
rules.body: Every semantic color is paired with a label, legend, or text token. Meaning must survive a grayscale render — required for color-vision accessibility.

rules.name: The Tabular-Numeral Rule
rules.body: Any figure that changes during the simulation uses tabular-nums in IBM Plex Mono or IBM Plex Sans Condensed, so digits hold their column and never jitter as inputs change.

rules.name: The One-Eyebrow Rule
rules.body: The mono uppercase eyebrow is a deliberate, singular instrument label in the hero, plus mono readout labels. It is not multiplied above every section; section identity comes from the leading gradient tick.

rules.name: The Layer-Before-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Reach for the next surface step before a shadow. Shadows respond to state and elevation; they are not the default way to separate content.

dos: Do keep the five semantic hues fixed: indigo = interactive/Business, violet = Enterprise/power-user, teal = pool (drains), amber = metered (runs), coral = limit (blocks).
Do render every changing figure in tabular-mono so digits don't shift during animation.
Do create depth by stepping the surface ramp (bg -> panel -> panel-2 -> panel-3) before adding a shadow.
Do pair every semantic color with a label, legend, or text token so meaning survives grayscale.
Do animate state with cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1) (0.15-0.18s for UI state, 0.6s for the meter), and keep the prefers-reduced-motion collapse intact.
Do treat the bill-composition meter as the signature — license + metered segments read against the coral ceiling.
donts: Don't build a generic SaaS hero-metric template (big number + gradient accent + supporting stats). The bill readout is an instrument, not a marketing metric.
Don't add identical icon-heading-text card grids, or a tracked uppercase eyebrow above every section.
Don't go flashy/consumer-marketing: no drenched heroes, no motion for spectacle.
Don't turn dense figures into a raw spreadsheet wall — keep them color-coded and guided.
Don't use background-clip: text gradient text; emphasize with weight, size, or a single solid color.
Don't use a border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't repurpose a semantic hue decoratively — no amber unless it means metered, no coral unless it means blocked.
